  "brief_explanation": "The increase in government expenditures needed to reach full employment is calculated using the formula: ΔG = (Yf - Ya) / (1 - MPC), where ΔG is the change in government spending, Yf is the full-employment GDP, Ya is the actual GDP, and MPC is the marginal propensity to consume. Plugging in the given values results in a need for an increase of 50 billion dollars."
}
